timeline.label: Q1 2026: net sales $1.495B (+14% YoY); operating income $215M; EPS $2.72 diluted
Acquired Elo Holdings for ~$1.30B cash, expanding self-service touchscreen and kiosk portfolio into Connected Frontline segment
Acquired Photoneo (~$62M) for 3D machine vision; results in Asset Visibility and Automation segment
Reorganized reportable segments to Connected Frontline (CF) and Asset Visibility and Automation (AVA), replacing prior EVM/AIT structure
FY2025 share repurchases $587M; cash balance fell to $125M as M&A and buybacks drew down liquidity
Completed Matrox Imaging acquisition, expanding machine vision and fixed industrial scanning in automation adjacency
William J. Burns became CEO, succeeding Anders Gustafsson; Burns had led product and solutions as Chief Product & Solutions Officer since 2015
Acquired Fetch Robotics, entering autonomous mobile robots for warehouse workflows
Acquired Motorola Solutions enterprise business, creating scale in mobile computing and barcode printing

industry.structure: Oligopolistic enterprise asset intelligence market led by Zebra, Honeywell, and Datalogic in mobile computing and automatic identification; high switching costs from validated workflows and installed base.
industry.share_shift: Zebra gaining share in machine vision and self-service via Matrox, Photoneo, and Elo tuck-ins; AVA organic growth turned positive (+7% in FY2025) after prior-year decline.
industry.trend: Mix shift toward services, software subscriptions, and AI-enabled frontline workflows; warehouse automation and RFID adoption remain tailwinds while retail capex cycles create volatility.
